rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LOUISE DOROTHY ZINGRONE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues to include: 1) Executor requests the Court to surcharge Gisele Follet's interest for her "inequitable and unconscionable claims" and to shift the litigation expenses to her. 2) Executor requests the Court to surcharge Gisele Follet's interest in the total amount of $4,935.60 she improperly withdrew while acting as POA. 3) Executor requests $12,912 in extraordinary fees. a. She requests $125/hr. b. She alleges that she has provided $45,193 in ordinary services, which is calculated at $125/hr. Her hours include ~30 hours incurred prior to her appointment and ~190 hours to travel from Wisconsin to handle items in storage. c. Court to determine whether sufficient showing has been made to allow extraordinary fees for Executor. 4) Attorney is requesting $48,300 in extraordinary fees. a. No court approval was sought to represent the estate in litigation as required under Local Rule 14.50. Attorney states that the Local Rule did not seem to apply to this situation. b. Attorney's time reported for ordinary services begins from 3/28/18; however, it does not appear that Executor ever filed a petition for probate (though it appears that she improperly attempted to amend Thomas Zingrone's petition), and she was appointed administrator on 12/4/18 by Gisele Follett's petition. It appears the first pleading that was filed by the attorney on behalf of Executor was on 1/15/19 (NB: the letters that were issued still show that attorney was representing Thomas Zingrone) and all prior services appear to have been provided to Thomas Zingrone, who was never appointed here. c. Court to determine whether extraordinary fees should be granted. d. $1,308.88 is left in client trust account, and award of fees will need to be paid from that account first. 5) Attorney was reimbursed $611.70 for filing the initial probate petition and related documents for Thomas Zingrone. It is unclear why the reimbursement was made, as it was Gisele Follet's petition that appointed Executor. 6) Over $3,000 was paid to Executor for estate travel between 6/5/19 to 6/9/19, most of which involved renting a moving truck, gas, and hotel to move estate personal property. Executor also reimbursed herself ~$1,100 for a trip taken between 3/24/22 to 3/27/19. Long distance travel is reimbursed only in the Court's discretion, depending upon the circumstances disclosed. 7) The accounting is out of balance by $111.88, which Executor and her attorney have not been able to reconcile. 8) Four separate objections have been filed by Gisele Follett. The objections (also called "Response") have not been properly verified (e.g. signed under penalty of perjury under the laws of the State of California that the pleading is true and correct). Remote appearances should be made whenever possible. Please review the 11th amended general order. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: WHEN ENTERING "YOUR NAME" ON ZOOM, BOTH THE FIRST AND LAST NAME, AS WELL THE CASE NUMBER, MUST BE INCLUDED. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833, and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Information regarding appearance by CourtCall can be found in the 11th amended general order.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE DEL PRADO FAMILY TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Remove Court Appointed Trustee For Trust Violations, Mismanagement Of Property And Trust Assets, Demand For Full Accounting On Proceeds Of Property Sale And For Damages Attributable To Trustees Malfeasance
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required on the sole issue of whether this petition should be taken off calendar for lack of progress and failure to cure procedural defects. The petition has been pending since November 2021. 1) No proof of timley service of notice of "Amendments to Petition" filed on 05/19/2022 has been filed. 2) No proof of serivice showing direct notice given to the trustee at his own address, instead of his attorney's, has been filed. 3) No proof of service of process on the buyer of the trust real property and all occupants, as a person other than a trustee or beneficiary whose right, title, or interest would be affected by the petition under Probate Code section 17203(b), has been filed. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DAVID ROBERT SENECHEK
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/22/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. After reviewing both the extraordinary timesheets and ordinary timesheets examiner is unable to determine any extraordinary services. The timesheets for ordinary and extraordinary appear to have identical entries, virtually identical entries, and/or the listed services do not appear extraordinary. Please file another declaration that states what entries are extraordinary and do not have an identical entry or virtually identical entry on the ordinary services. Please explain the legal basis for the court awarding extraordinary fees when both the ordinary and extraordinary entries appear to have idential entries and/or virtually identical entries. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 8/8/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DENIS BOUVIER
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/8/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1) Please explain petition page 2 lines 18-20, "Petitioner was unaware that Notice of Proposed Action was required at the time the transfers below were made and was advised by a prior estate attorney that such transfers were reasonable and timely." It appears the only attorneys of record in this matter since the initial filing on March 11, 2021 are Vanessa Sundin and Yulissa Zulaica. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 8/1/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

ISSUES (may be addressed at hearing): 1) Compliance status hearing was set for 07/18/2022 for filling the listed documents. Page 24 of the California probate referee manual says, "the vested interest of the estate in the right to receive distribution from the probate estate or trust or other entity should be inventoried as a single item." PES-19-303120 Estate of Krya Mary Baele - The petition alleges $304,033.60 is to be distributed to Jeffrey Kazor, Guardian of the Estate of Tristan Kazor a. A receipt for the funds placed in a blocked account along with a request to waive accountings. b. The Inventory and Appraisal with proof of mailing of the Inventory and Appraisal or Order Dispending with Mailing c. Proof of Mailing of notice of how to file an objection to the Inventory and Appraisal d. The $800.00 Assessment fee for the court investigation shall be paid within 90 days of this order.

rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing. No conformed copy of signed order will be returned (no self-addressed stamped envelope provided). Petition for final distribution or petition for approval of report of status of administration must be filed by 07/24/2023. If not on file, appearance required at status hearing set for 08/07/2023.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF SIDNEY UNOBSKEY
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 08/08/2022 (no earlier date available), as most issues e-mailed to counsel on 06/20/2022 remain unaddressed. Note: Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GLORIA JEAN WASH RILEY
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 08/15/2022 (no earlier date available). Notes were e-mailed to counsel on 07/11/2022. Note: Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JAMIE ALISSA AVERILL HARVEY
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 08/01/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed with a cover page, separately, and confidentially. Do not file form DE-147S as an attachment to the Duties and Liabilities form.[LRSF 14.18 E.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ROBERT ALBERT GARZONA
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF BARRY MARTIN MCGRATH
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing. status hearing on close of estate set for 7/24/2023. Amy Harrington is appointed probate referee.
